sourceEvidence.label: Femoral Neck Fracture — Learning objectives
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Learning objectives for Femoral Neck Fracture

objectives: Explain intracapsular anatomy and blood supply risk after femoral neck fracture.
Differentiate fixation versus arthroplasty pathways by age, displacement, and function.
Communicate AVN and nonunion risks with appropriate urgency.

sourceEvidence.label: Femoral Neck Fracture — Deep study template
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Deep study template for Femoral Neck Fracture

decisionMaking: Age, baseline function, displacement, head viability, medical risk
boardPearls: Displaced femoral neck fractures in older adults are often arthroplasty problems

sourceEvidence.label: Femoral Neck Fracture — Fast study template
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Fast study template for Femoral Neck Fracture

payload.oneLiner: Femoral neck fracture is an intracapsular injury where displacement and blood supply determine fixation versus arthroplasty.
pimpQuestions: Why are displaced femoral neck fractures different?
What changes management in younger patients?
orSurvivalTips: Mention DVT prophylaxis and AVN surveillance when relevant

extractedSignals.summary: 9 high-confidence Anki card mapping(s) for curriculum node trauma-femoral-neck-fractures
64 Orthobullets question mapping(s) for trauma-femoral-neck-fractures (metadata only — no stems/answers)

metadata.description: Intracapsular proximal femur fracture with head viability and AVN risk concerns.
Parallel screw fixation for selected femoral neck fracture patterns.
Failure of femoral neck fracture healing with mechanical symptoms.
Open reduction internal fixation of femoral neck fracture.

relationship_metadata.note: age and function dependent — attending review required

metadata.claim_text: Displaced femoral neck fractures in older adults are often arthroplasty problems rather than screw fixation problems.
Applying geriatric arthroplasty defaults to young active patients with femoral neck fractures without head-preservation discussion.
Garden displacement language helps communicate femoral neck fracture stability and treatment tendency.
State fracture location, displacement, medical optimization urgency, DVT prophylaxis, and likely fixation versus arthroplasty pathway.

metadata.action: Urgent anatomic reduction and stable fixation (typically cannulated screws) with AVN surveillance plan
metadata.trigger: Younger patient with displaced or non-displaced femoral neck fracture where head preservation is prioritized

metadata.action: Discuss hemiarthroplasty versus total hip arthroplasty with attending; document medical optimization and DVT prophylaxis
metadata.trigger: Displaced femoral neck fracture in older adult with low functional reserve or poor healing potential

metadata.description: Partial hip replacement for select displaced femoral neck fractures in older adults.
Displacement-based femoral neck fracture classification framework.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Displaced Femoral Neck Fracture

metadata.description: Radiographic displacement pattern altering fixation versus arthroplasty calculus.
Femoral head osteonecrosis after compromised retinacular blood supply.

metadata.claim_text: Medial femoral circumflex and retinacular blood supply vulnerability explains AVN risk after femoral neck fracture.
Femoral neck fractures are intracapsular injuries where fracture location and displacement drive fixation versus arthroplasty decisions.
Femoral neck nonunion remains a major failure mode when reduction, fixation, or biology are unfavorable.
